apiKey.description: Amplitude project API key. You can find this key in the "General" tab of your Amplitude project.
apiKey.label: API Key

secretKey.description: Amplitude project secret key. You can find this key in the "General" tab of your Amplitude project.
secretKey.label: Secret Key

endpoint.description: The region to send your data.
endpoint.label: Endpoint Region

actions.name: Group Identify User
actions.description: Set or update properties of particular groups. Note that these updates will only affect events going forward.

fields.label: User ID
fields.description: A UUID (unique user ID) specified by you. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event. If either user ID or device ID is present, an associate user to group call will be made.

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: A UUID (unique user ID) specified by you. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event. If either user ID or device ID is present, an associate user to group call will be made.

fields.label: Device ID
fields.description: A device specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor (IDFV) on iOS. If either user ID or device ID is present, an associate user to group call will be made.

device_id.title: Device ID
device_id.description: A device specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor (IDFV) on iOS. If either user ID or device ID is present, an associate user to group call will be made.

fields.label: Insert ID
fields.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

insert_id.title: Insert ID
insert_id.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

fields.label: Timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

time.title: Timestamp
time.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

fields.label: Group Properties
fields.description: Additional data tied to the group in Amplitude.

group_properties.title: Group Properties
group_properties.description: Additional data tied to the group in Amplitude.

fields.label: Group Type
fields.description: The type of the group

fields.label: Group Value
fields.description: The value of the group

fields.label: Minimum ID Length
fields.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

min_id_length.title: Minimum ID Length
min_id_length.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

actions.name: Identify User
actions.description: Set the user ID for a particular device ID or update user properties without sending an event to Amplitude.

fields.label: User ID
fields.description: A UUID (unique user ID) specified by you. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event. Required unless device ID is present.

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: A UUID (unique user ID) specified by you. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event. Required unless device ID is present.

fields.label: Device ID
fields.description: A device specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor (IDFV) on iOS. Required unless user ID is present.

device_id.title: Device ID
device_id.description: A device specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor (IDFV) on iOS. Required unless user ID is present.

fields.label: User Properties
fields.description: Additional data tied to the user in Amplitude. Each distinct value will show up as a user segment on the Amplitude dashboard. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers. **Note:** You can store property values in an array and date values are transformed into string values.

user_properties.title: User Properties
user_properties.description: Additional data tied to the user in Amplitude. Each distinct value will show up as a user segment on the Amplitude dashboard. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers. **Note:** You can store property values in an array and date values are transformed into string values.

fields.label: Groups
fields.description: Groups of users for Amplitude's account-level reporting feature. Note: You can only track up to 5 groups. Any groups past that threshold will not be tracked. **Note:** This feature is only available to Amplitude Enterprise customers who have purchased the Amplitude Accounts add-on.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Groups of users for Amplitude's account-level reporting feature. Note: You can only track up to 5 groups. Any groups past that threshold will not be tracked. **Note:** This feature is only available to Amplitude Enterprise customers who have purchased the Amplitude Accounts add-on.

fields.label: Insert ID
fields.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

insert_id.title: Insert ID
insert_id.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

fields.label: User Agent

fields.label: User Agent Parsing
fields.description: Enabling this setting will set the Device manufacturer, Device Model and OS Name properties based on the user agent string provided in the userAgent field

fields.label: UTM Properties
fields.description: UTM Tracking Properties

fields.description: The referrer of the web request. Sent to Amplitude as both last touch “referrer” and first touch “initial_referrer”

referrer.description: The referrer of the web request. Sent to Amplitude as both last touch “referrer” and first touch “initial_referrer”

fields.label: Minimum ID Length
fields.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

min_id_length.title: Minimum ID Length
min_id_length.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

fields.description: The name of the library that generated the event.

fields.label: User Agent Data
fields.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

userAgentData.title: User Agent Data
userAgentData.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

fields.label: User ID
fields.description: A readable ID specified by you. Must have a minimum length of 5 characters. Required unless device ID is present. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event.

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: A readable ID specified by you. Must have a minimum length of 5 characters. Required unless device ID is present. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event.

fields.label: Device ID
fields.description: A device-specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor on iOS. Required unless user ID is present. If a device ID is not sent with the event, it will be set to a hashed version of the user ID.

device_id.title: Device ID
device_id.description: A device-specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor on iOS. Required unless user ID is present. If a device ID is not sent with the event, it will be set to a hashed version of the user ID.

fields.label: Event Type
fields.description: A unique identifier for your event.

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: A unique identifier for your event.

fields.label: Session ID
fields.description: The start time of the session, necessary if you want to associate events with a particular system. To use automatic Amplitude session tracking in browsers, enable Analytics 2.0 on your connected source.

session_id.title: Session ID
session_id.description: The start time of the session, necessary if you want to associate events with a particular system. To use automatic Amplitude session tracking in browsers, enable Analytics 2.0 on your connected source.

fields.label: Timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

time.title: Timestamp
time.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

fields.label: Event Properties
fields.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

event_properties.title: Event Properties
event_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

fields.label: User Properties
fields.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data tied to the user. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

user_properties.title: User Properties
user_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data tied to the user. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

fields.label: Groups
fields.description: Groups of users for the event as an event-level group. You can only track up to 5 groups. **Note:** This Amplitude feature is only available to Enterprise customers who have purchased the Accounts add-on.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Groups of users for the event as an event-level group. You can only track up to 5 groups. **Note:** This Amplitude feature is only available to Enterprise customers who have purchased the Accounts add-on.

fields.description: Platform of the device. If using analytics.js to send events from a Browser and no if no Platform value is provided, the value "Web" will be sent.

fields.label: Designated Market Area
fields.description: The current Designated Market Area of the user.

dma.description: The current Designated Market Area of the user.

fields.label: Language
fields.description: The language set by the user.

language.title: Language
language.description: The language set by the user.

fields.label: Price
fields.description: The price of the item purchased. Required for revenue data if the revenue field is not sent. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

price.title: Price
price.description: The price of the item purchased. Required for revenue data if the revenue field is not sent. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.description: The quantity of the item purchased. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

quantity.title: Quantity
quantity.description: The quantity of the item purchased. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

fields.label: Revenue
fields.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds. **Note:** You will need to explicitly set this if you are using the Amplitude in cloud-mode.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds. **Note:** You will need to explicitly set this if you are using the Amplitude in cloud-mode.

fields.label: Product ID
fields.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

productId.title: Product ID
productId.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Revenue Type
fields.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

revenueType.title: Revenue Type
revenueType.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Latitude
fields.description: The current Latitude of the user.

location_lat.title: Latitude
location_lat.description: The current Latitude of the user.

fields.label: Longtitude
fields.description: The current Longitude of the user.

location_lng.title: Longtitude
location_lng.description: The current Longitude of the user.

fields.label: IP Address
fields.description: The IP address of the user. Use "$remote" to use the IP address on the upload request. Amplitude will use the IP address to reverse lookup a user's location (city, country, region, and DMA). Amplitude has the ability to drop the location and IP address from events once it reaches our servers. You can submit a request to Amplitude's platform specialist team here to configure this for you.

ip.title: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the user. Use "$remote" to use the IP address on the upload request. Amplitude will use the IP address to reverse lookup a user's location (city, country, region, and DMA). Amplitude has the ability to drop the location and IP address from events once it reaches our servers. You can submit a request to Amplitude's platform specialist team here to configure this for you.

fields.label: Identifier For Advertiser (IDFA)
fields.description: Identifier for Advertiser. _(iOS)_

fields.label: Identifier For Vendor (IDFV)
fields.description: Identifier for Vendor. _(iOS)_

fields.label: Google Play Services Advertising ID
fields.description: Google Play Services advertising ID. _(Android)_

fields.label: Android ID
fields.description: Android ID (not the advertising ID). _(Android)_

fields.label: Event ID
fields.description: An incrementing counter to distinguish events with the same user ID and timestamp from each other. Amplitude recommends you send an event ID, increasing over time, especially if you expect events to occur simultanenously.

event_id.title: Event ID
event_id.description: An incrementing counter to distinguish events with the same user ID and timestamp from each other. Amplitude recommends you send an event ID, increasing over time, especially if you expect events to occur simultanenously.

fields.label: Insert ID
fields.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

insert_id.title: Insert ID
insert_id.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

fields.label: HIDDEN: Library
fields.description: HIDDEN: The name of the library that generated the event.

library.title: HIDDEN: Library
library.description: HIDDEN: The name of the library that generated the event.

fields.label: Library Settings
fields.description: Specifies the "library" value to send to Amplitude. Select "Use Mapping" to set the value from the "Library Mapping" field. "Legacy Behaviour" sets the value to "segment".

library2.title: Library Settings
library2.description: Specifies the "library" value to send to Amplitude. Select "Use Mapping" to set the value from the "Library Mapping" field. "Legacy Behaviour" sets the value to "segment".

behavior.title: Behavior
behavior.description: Configure how to set the library value.

mapping.title: Library Mapping
mapping.description: The library value to send to Amplitude. Only used when the "Behavior" field is set to "Use Mapping".

fields.label: Products
fields.description: The list of products purchased.

price.title: Price
price.description: The price of the item purchased. Required for revenue data if the revenue field is not sent. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

quantity.title: Quantity
quantity.description: The quantity of the item purchased. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

productId.title: Product ID
productId.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

revenueType.title: Revenue Type
revenueType.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Use Batch Endpoint
fields.description: If true, events are sent to Amplitude's `batch` endpoint rather than their `httpapi` events endpoint. Enabling this setting may help reduce 429s – or throttling errors – from Amplitude. More information about Amplitude's throttling is available in [their docs](https://developers.amplitude.com/docs/batch-event-upload-api#429s-in-depth).

use_batch_endpoint.title: Use Batch Endpoint
use_batch_endpoint.description: If true, events are sent to Amplitude's `batch` endpoint rather than their `httpapi` events endpoint. Enabling this setting may help reduce 429s – or throttling errors – from Amplitude. More information about Amplitude's throttling is available in [their docs](https://developers.amplitude.com/docs/batch-event-upload-api#429s-in-depth).

dma.title: Designated Market Area

fields.label: Price
fields.description: The price of the item purchased. Required for revenue data if the revenue field is not sent. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

price.title: Price
price.description: The price of the item purchased. Required for revenue data if the revenue field is not sent. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.description: The quantity of the item purchased. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

quantity.title: Quantity
quantity.description: The quantity of the item purchased. Defaults to 1 if not specified.

fields.label: Revenue
fields.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds. **Note:** You will need to explicitly set this if you are using the Amplitude in cloud-mode.

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds. **Note:** You will need to explicitly set this if you are using the Amplitude in cloud-mode.

fields.label: Product ID
fields.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

productId.title: Product ID
productId.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Revenue Type
fields.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

revenueType.title: Revenue Type
revenueType.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Latitude
fields.description: The current Latitude of the user.

location_lat.title: Latitude
location_lat.description: The current Latitude of the user.

fields.label: Longtitude
fields.description: The current Longitude of the user.

location_lng.title: Longtitude
location_lng.description: The current Longitude of the user.

fields.label: IP Address
fields.description: The IP address of the user. Use "$remote" to use the IP address on the upload request. Amplitude will use the IP address to reverse lookup a user's location (city, country, region, and DMA). Amplitude has the ability to drop the location and IP address from events once it reaches our servers. You can submit a request to Amplitude's platform specialist team here to configure this for you.

ip.title: IP Address
ip.description: The IP address of the user. Use "$remote" to use the IP address on the upload request. Amplitude will use the IP address to reverse lookup a user's location (city, country, region, and DMA). Amplitude has the ability to drop the location and IP address from events once it reaches our servers. You can submit a request to Amplitude's platform specialist team here to configure this for you.

fields.label: Identifier For Advertiser (IDFA)
fields.description: Identifier for Advertiser. _(iOS)_

fields.label: Identifier For Vendor (IDFV)

fields.description: Google Play Services advertising ID. _(Android)_

fields.label: Android ID
fields.description: Android ID (not the advertising ID). _(Android)_

fields.label: Event ID
fields.description: An incrementing counter to distinguish events with the same user ID and timestamp from each other. Amplitude recommends you send an event ID, increasing over time, especially if you expect events to occur simultanenously.

event_id.title: Event ID
event_id.description: An incrementing counter to distinguish events with the same user ID and timestamp from each other. Amplitude recommends you send an event ID, increasing over time, especially if you expect events to occur simultanenously.

fields.label: Insert ID
fields.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

insert_id.title: Insert ID
insert_id.description: Amplitude will deduplicate subsequent events sent with this ID we have already seen before within the past 7 days. Amplitude recommends generating a UUID or using some combination of device ID, user ID, event type, event ID, and time.

fields.label: HIDDEN: Library
fields.description: HIDDEN: The name of the library that generated the event.

library.title: HIDDEN: Library
library.description: HIDDEN: The name of the library that generated the event.

fields.label: Library Settings
fields.description: Specifies the "library" value to send to Amplitude. Select "Use Mapping" to set the value from the "Library Mapping" field. "Legacy Behaviour" sets the value to "segment".

library2.title: Library Settings
library2.description: Specifies the "library" value to send to Amplitude. Select "Use Mapping" to set the value from the "Library Mapping" field. "Legacy Behaviour" sets the value to "segment".

behavior.title: Behavior
behavior.description: Configure how to set the library value.

mapping.title: Library Mapping
mapping.description: The library value to send to Amplitude. Only used when the "Behavior" field is set to "Use Mapping".

fields.label: Products
fields.description: The list of products purchased.

price.title: Price

quantity.title: Quantity

revenue.title: Revenue
revenue.description: Revenue = price * quantity. If you send all 3 fields of price, quantity, and revenue, then (price * quantity) will be used as the revenue value. You can use negative values to indicate refunds.

productId.title: Product ID
productId.description: An identifier for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

revenueType.title: Revenue Type
revenueType.description: The type of revenue for the item purchased. You must send a price and quantity or revenue with this field.

fields.label: Set Once
fields.description: The following fields will only be set as user properties if they do not already have a value.

setOnce.title: Set Once
setOnce.description: The following fields will only be set as user properties if they do not already have a value.

initial_referrer.title: Initial Referrer
initial_referrer.description: The referrer of the web request.

fields.label: Set Always
fields.description: The following fields will be set as user properties for every event.

setAlways.title: Set Always
setAlways.description: The following fields will be set as user properties for every event.

fields.label: Add
fields.description: Increment a user property by a number with add. If the user property doesn't have a value set yet, it's initialized to 0.

add.title: Add
add.description: Increment a user property by a number with add. If the user property doesn't have a value set yet, it's initialized to 0.

fields.label: Use Batch Endpoint
fields.description: If true, events are sent to Amplitude's `batch` endpoint rather than their `httpapi` events endpoint. Enabling this setting may help reduce 429s – or throttling errors – from Amplitude. More information about Amplitude's throttling is available in [their docs](https://developers.amplitude.com/docs/batch-event-upload-api#429s-in-depth).

use_batch_endpoint.title: Use Batch Endpoint
use_batch_endpoint.description: If true, events are sent to Amplitude's `batch` endpoint rather than their `httpapi` events endpoint. Enabling this setting may help reduce 429s – or throttling errors – from Amplitude. More information about Amplitude's throttling is available in [their docs](https://developers.amplitude.com/docs/batch-event-upload-api#429s-in-depth).

fields.label: User Agent
fields.description: The user agent of the device sending the event.

userAgent.title: User Agent
userAgent.description: The user agent of the device sending the event.

fields.label: User Agent Parsing
fields.description: Enabling this setting will set the Device manufacturer, Device Model and OS Name properties based on the user agent string provided in the userAgent field.

userAgentParsing.title: User Agent Parsing
userAgentParsing.description: Enabling this setting will set the Device manufacturer, Device Model and OS Name properties based on the user agent string provided in the userAgent field.

fields.label: Include Raw User Agent
fields.description: Enabling this setting will send user_agent based on the raw user agent string provided in the userAgent field

includeRawUserAgent.title: Include Raw User Agent
includeRawUserAgent.description: Enabling this setting will send user_agent based on the raw user agent string provided in the userAgent field

fields.label: Minimum ID Length
fields.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

min_id_length.title: Minimum ID Length
min_id_length.description: Amplitude has a default minimum id length of 5 characters for user_id and device_id fields. This field allows the minimum to be overridden to allow shorter id lengths.

fields.label: User Agent Data
fields.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

userAgentData.title: User Agent Data
userAgentData.description: The user agent data of device sending the event

actions.name: Log Purchase
actions.description: Send purchase/revenue events to Amplitude. Sends one main event with the original event type and total revenue, plus additional "Product Purchased" events for each product in the order.

fields.label: Track Revenue Per Product
fields.description: When enabled, track revenue with each product within the event. When disabled, track total revenue once for the event.

trackRevenuePerProduct.title: Track Revenue Per Product
trackRevenuePerProduct.description: When enabled, track revenue with each product within the event. When disabled, track total revenue once for the event.

fields.label: User ID
fields.description: A readable ID specified by you. Must have a minimum length of 5 characters. Required unless device ID is present. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event.

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: A readable ID specified by you. Must have a minimum length of 5 characters. Required unless device ID is present. **Note:** If you send a request with a user ID that is not in the Amplitude system yet, then the user tied to that ID will not be marked new until their first event.

fields.label: Device ID
fields.description: A device-specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor on iOS. Required unless user ID is present. If a device ID is not sent with the event, it will be set to a hashed version of the user ID.

device_id.title: Device ID
device_id.description: A device-specific identifier, such as the Identifier for Vendor on iOS. Required unless user ID is present. If a device ID is not sent with the event, it will be set to a hashed version of the user ID.

fields.label: Event Type
fields.description: A unique identifier for your event.

event_type.title: Event Type
event_type.description: A unique identifier for your event.

fields.label: Session ID
fields.description: The start time of the session, necessary if you want to associate events with a particular system. To use automatic Amplitude session tracking in browsers, enable Analytics 2.0 on your connected source.

session_id.title: Session ID
session_id.description: The start time of the session, necessary if you want to associate events with a particular system. To use automatic Amplitude session tracking in browsers, enable Analytics 2.0 on your connected source.

fields.label: Timestamp
fields.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

time.title: Timestamp
time.description: The timestamp of the event. If time is not sent with the event, it will be set to the request upload time.

fields.label: Event Properties
fields.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

event_properties.title: Event Properties
event_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data to be sent along with the event. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

fields.label: User Properties
fields.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data tied to the user. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

user_properties.title: User Properties
user_properties.description: An object of key-value pairs that represent additional data tied to the user. You can store property values in an array, but note that Amplitude only supports one-dimensional arrays. Date values are transformed into string values. Object depth may not exceed 40 layers.

fields.label: Groups
fields.description: Groups of users for the event as an event-level group. You can only track up to 5 groups. **Note:** This Amplitude feature is only available to Enterprise customers who have purchased the Accounts add-on.

groups.title: Groups
groups.description: Groups of users for the event as an event-level group. You can only track up to 5 groups. **Note:** This Amplitude feature is only available to Enterprise customers who have purchased the Accounts add-on.

fields.label: App Version

app_version.title: App Version
app_version.description: The current version of your application.

fields.label: Platform
fields.description: Platform of the device. If using analytics.js to send events from a Browser and no if no Platform value is provided, the value "Web" will be sent.

actions.name: Session Plugin
actions.description: Generates a Session ID and attaches it to every Amplitude browser based event.
actions.defaultTrigger: type = "track" or type = "identify" or type = "group" or type = "page" or type = "alias"

fields.label: Session Length
fields.description: Time in milliseconds to be used before considering a session stale.

sessionLength.title: Session Length
sessionLength.description: Time in milliseconds to be used before considering a session stale.

fields.label: Allow Session Tracking
fields.description: if set to true, 'Session Started' and 'Session Ended' events will be triggered from the user's browser. These events will be forwarded to all connected Destinations.

triggerSessionEvents.title: Allow Session Tracking
triggerSessionEvents.description: if set to true, 'Session Started' and 'Session Ended' events will be triggered from the user's browser. These events will be forwarded to all connected Destinations.

fields.label: Session Start Event
fields.description: The event name to use for the session start event.

fields.label: Session End Event

sessionEndEvent.title: Session End Event
